report_metadata.report_title: AA25-266A CISA Shares Lessons Learned from an Incident Response Engagement

tools.name: Burp Suite
tools.evidence_snippet: The cyber threat actors identified CVE-2024-36401 in the organization's public-facing GeoServer using Burp Suite (Burp Scanner)

tools.name: China Chopper
tools.evidence_snippet: they uploaded or attempted to upload web shells such as China Chopper

tools.name: dirtycow
tools.evidence_snippet: The cyber threat actors attempted to escalate privileges with the publicly available dirtycow tool

tools.name: fscan
tools.evidence_snippet: they downloaded the fscan tool to scan the organization's network

tools.name: Stowaway
tools.evidence_snippet: The cyber threat actors used Stowaway, a publicly available multi-level proxy tool, to establish C2

tools.name: RingQ
tools.evidence_snippet: CISA also observed files on GeoServer 1 named RinqQ.exe and RingQ.rar, which likely refer to a publicly available defense evasion tool called RingQ

cves.evidence_snippet: cyber threat actors compromised the agency by exploiting CVE-2024-36401 in a GeoServer
The cyber threat actors attempted to escalate privileges with the publicly available dirtycow tool, which can be used to exploit CVE-2016-5195

ttps.evidence_snippet: The cyber threat actors performed active scanning to identify vulnerabilities they could use for initial access
everaged a commercially available virtual private server (VPS) from a cloud infrastructure provider [T1583.003]
the cyber threat actors exploited CVE 2024-36401 [T1190]
The cyber threat actors primarily used web shells [T1505.003] on internet-facing hosts
along with cron jobs (scheduled commands that run automatically at specified times) [T1053.003]
valid accounts [T1078] for persistence. CISA also identified
employed indirect command execution via .php web shells and xp_cmdshell [T1202]
abused Background Intelligence Transfer Service (BITS) jobs [T1197].
cyber threat actors attempted to escalate privileges with the publicly available dirtycow tool,1F 2 which can be used to exploit CVE-2016-5195 [CWE-362: “Race Condition”] [T1068]s
relied on brute force techniques [T1110] to obtain passwords for lateral movement and privilege escalation
They performed ping sweeps of hosts within specific subnets [T1018] and downloaded
env ps -aux ipconfig [T1016] date who -b rpm -qa polkit
rpm -qa polkit netstat -ano [T1049] Additionally, they employed LOTL techniques for user, service, filesystem, and network discovery
cat /etc/passwd [T1087.001]
cat /etc/redhat-release [T1082]
rpm -qa polkit netstat -ano [T1049] Additionally, they employed LOTL techniques for user, service, filesystem, and network discovery
whoami [T1033]
tasklist [T1057]
dir c: [T1083]
The cyber threat actors used a connection proxy to direct traffic from their C2 server
The cyber threat actors used a connection proxy to direct traffic from their C2 server
The cyber threat actors used PowerShell and bitsadmin getfile to download payloads

summary: CISA incident response at U.S. FCEB agency following EDR detection on July 31, 2024. Cyber threat actors exploited CVE-2024-36401 (GeoServer RCE) on July 11 and July 24, 2024, for initial access to two GeoServers, moved laterally to Web Server and SQL Server using China Chopper web shells, fscan network scanning, dirtycow privilege escalation, Stowaway C2 proxy tool, and LOTL techniques. Three-week undetected dwell time. No threat actor attribution. Lessons learned: patch promptly, test IRPs, review EDR alerts continuously.

timeline.evidence_snippet: July 11, 2024 GeoServer 1 Initial Access to GeoServer 1

timeline.event: CVE-2024-36401 added to CISA KEV Catalog
timeline.evidence_snippet: July 15, 2024 CVE-2024-36401 added to CISA's Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Catalog

timeline.event: EDR detects Stowaway tool on GeoServer 1 (alert not observed by SOC)
timeline.evidence_snippet: July 15, 2024 GeoServer 1 EDR detects Stowaway tool on GeoServer 1
July 24, 2024 GeoServer 2 Initial Access to GeoServer 2
July 31, 2024 Web Server Initial Access to Web Server
July 31, 2024 SQL Server Initial Access to SQL Server

timeline.event: Organization observes SQL Alert and contains SQL Server and GeoServer 1
timeline.evidence_snippet: Aug. 1, 2024 Organization observes SQL Alert and contains SQL Server and GeoServer 1
Aug. 1, 2024 The impacted organization requested assistance from CISA
Aug. 5, 2024 CISA began forensic artifact analysis

timeline.event: Last observed threat actor activity on GeoServer 2
timeline.evidence_snippet: Aug. 6, 2024 Last observed threat actors activity—discovery commands on GeoServer 2
